chunk_summary: This section focuses on preventing choking in infants and young children under 2 years during feeding, emphasizing safe food choices and practices tailored to their developmental stage.
learning_objectives: Identify foods that pose choking risks for children under 2 years.
Understand safe feeding practices to minimize choking hazards.
Learn developmental factors affecting swallowing and chewing in infants.
Recognize emergency responses for choking incidents in young children.
key_facts: Children under 2 years are at higher risk of choking due to immature chewing and swallowing abilities.
Avoid small, hard, round, or sticky foods such as nuts, grapes, and chunks of meat for children under 2 years.
Cut food into small, manageable pieces (e.g., quarter grapes or shred meat) to reduce choking risks.
Supervise infants and young children during feeding to ensure safe eating practices.
Introduce age-appropriate textures gradually as the child develops chewing skills.
reference_quote: Supervision during feeding is critical to prevent choking, especially in children under 2 years who are still developing chewing and swallowing skills.
document_key_topics: Safe food preparation for young children
